verdict: The chart has good physical flow and structural ideas, but the excessive use of off-beat syncopation and tight hold releases makes it far too complex and punishing for an Easy difficulty.

issues.problem: Frequent use of off-beat (8th-note syncopated) steps. Easy charts should strictly adhere to the main quarter-note beats to remain readable for beginners.
Tight hold-to-tap transitions. The hold ends only half a beat (0.19s) before the next tap, which doesn't give beginner players enough time to release and reposition.
An 8th-note burst (L -> R -> D) at 152 BPM near the end. This is a sudden difficulty spike that is inappropriate for an Easy difficulty level.
